title: Chief of Section, P-5, New York Liaison Office
content: The position is responsible for the UNDRR New York Liaison Office and reports to the Chief of Intergovernmental Processes, Interagency Cooperation and Partnerships Branch, who is based in Geneva.

title: National Consultant – Development of Localization Model (Manila, Philippines)
content: ADPC is seeking a Manila-based national consultant to develop and document a localization model for disaster preparedness and climate resilience in the Philippines.

title: Reclaim Finance
content: Reclaim Finance is a non-governmental research and campaigning organization. It was founded in March 2020 by Lucie Pinson, winner of the 2020 Goldman Environmental Prize, with the aim of making finance work for the climate.

title: New model to forecast space weather on way
content: Aberystwyth scientists are developing a new model using coronagraph data to map the Sun’s magnetic field more accurately, improving forecasts of solar storms that threaten satellites, power grids and communications.

title: Slope stabilisation and modernisation of road infrastructure at Gródek nad Dunajcem, Poland
content: A complex engineering intervention secured slopes at risk of landslides in the Małopolska region, Poland. It ensures the continued operation of road transport in areas affected by increasing intense rainfall due to climate change.

title: Bridging the last mile: Rethinking disaster risk communication in academic research
content: Risk communication is the bridge between science and preparedness. If academic research continues to prioritise prediction without equally focusing on how warnings are understood and acted upon, early warning systems will remain socially ineffective.

title: Integrated National Financing Framework (INFF)
content: The INFF Facility is a joint flagship initiative that responds to the demand for support from countries developing integrated national financing frameworks (INFFs)

title: Adapt or die: Why progressives need to deal with extreme weather
content: This report outlines how increasingly frequent and severe extreme weather events - flooding, storms, heatwaves and wildfires - are already undermining the UK’s public services, economic stability and political cohesion.

title: Failure to prepare for extreme weather risks losing a flood of public support, warns IPPR
content: The IPPR warns that failing to prepare for extreme weather is fuelling public anger and empowering populists. Most voters think the UK is unprepared, creating a mandate for policies that pair climate adaptation with emissions cuts.

title: Definitions and taxonomy for High Impact Low Probability (HILP) and outlier events
content: This study outlines a comprehensive effort to clarify and standardise how High Impact Low Probability (HILP) and outlier events are defined and categorised, with the aim of strengthening disaster risk reduction across sectors.

title: Validating disaster and climate resilience: how to create a gold standard for resilience-measurement
content: As climate risks intensify, fostering community resilience has become a global priority but a fundamental question remains for practice and policy: how can resilience be defined, measured, and proven to inform implementation?

title: Building climate-resilient agriculture in Europe: An economic perspective
content: This briefing links climate hazards to farm vulnerability and economic impacts and synthesises quantified findings from European farm-level case studies to demonstrate that climate-resilient agriculture is a economic imperative for Europe’s food security.

title: National consultant – Development of localization model (Vientiane, Lao PDR)
content: The purpose of this consultancy is to facilitate the development and documentation of a country (i.e., LDPP)-specific Localization Model, capturing how localization has been defined, operationalized, and institutionalized through the partnership.

title: National consultant – Development of localization model (Phnom Penh, Cambodia)
content: The purpose of this consultancy is to facilitate the development and documentation of a country (i.e., PPC)-specific Localization Model, capturing how localization has been defined, operationalized, and institutionalized through the partnership.

title: NOAA strengthens commitment to world-class tsunami monitoring and detection in Pacific
content: An agreement between NOAA, the Alaska Mesonet and the seismic network operated by the University of Alaska-Fairbanks Alaska Earthquake Center (UAF AEC) is funding nine AEC-owned seismic stations that support near-real-time tsunami threat information.

title: Strengthening cholera control through simulation-based training with Zambia’s water utilities
content: Organising preparedness training for water utilities in relation to cholera is a critical step in preventing and reducing outbreaks. It is essential because cholera is waterborne.

title: Where humanitarian action meets long-term water security
content: Rosine Kouame, an Ivorian water management expert with the Dutch Disaster Risk Reduction and Surge Support (DRRS) programme, led a team to address urgent local water needs while creating sustainable improvements for the community.

title: Clustering-based AI forecasts river water levels using just a few long records
content: Accurate forecasting via early warning systems is a highly useful technique for flood mitigation, agricultural irrigation, ecosystem and environmental sustainability, and numerous other applications.

title: Jeonbuk National University
content: Jeonbuk National University is one of ten Flagship Korean National Universities founded in 1947, located in Jeonju, South Korea.

title: 5 insights into understanding glacial lake outburst floods in a warming world
content: As glaciers retreat, glacial lakes grow and flood risks rise. Learn more about Glacial Lake Outburst Floods (GLOFs) in this article.

title: Raising awareness of 23 avoidable deaths
content: This publication synthesises evidence on twenty‑three major causes of mortality that are predictable, preventable, and treatable across low‑ and middle‑income countries (LMIC).

title: The water crisis we can’t see is still growing
content: A hidden water crisis is growing underground. As aquifers decline, drinking water, agriculture and global food systems face rising risk.

title: Comprehensive multi-hazard risk assessment in data-scarce regions – a study focused on Burundi
content: This study develops a comprehensive multi-hazard risk assessment framework for data-scarce environments, using Burundi as a case study to support disaster risk reduction and decision-making.

title: Review on tsunami research and risk mitigation: from prediction models to resilient coastal communities
content: This review synthesizes advances in multi-source observations, signal detection, propagation and inundation modeling, physics–AI hybrid prediction, and real-time warning workflows.

title: Megafires in Mediterranean Europe: the compound role of fire weather and drought
content: This study investigates why some wildfires in Mediterranean Europe escalate into very large fires or megafires, while others remain smaller.

title: Wagamama and embodied gender norms in Japan: cultural drivers of disaster vulnerability
content: This article examines how embodied gendered norms in Japan, especially those associated with women’s self-restraint and fear of being labelled Wagamama, exacerbate their vulnerability during disasters.

title: 5 ways climate change is driving up the cost of living
content: Learn how climate change contributes to the rise in the cost of living.

title: Pacific Prepared Podcast: The challenges of sending a weather report from one of Fiji’s most remote islands
content: The challenges of sending a weather report from one of Fiji’s most remote islands.

title: Fishers in the ‘Fishbowl of the Philippines’ question their future in these waters
content: Typhoons and the increasing presence of commercial vessels are pushing fishers in western Philippines’ Malampaya Sound to the margins.

title: New Savai‘i emergency operations centre breaks ground to strengthen island wide disaster response
content: Samoa has begun construction of the Savai‘i Sub-National Emergency Operations Centre (NEOC), marked by a groundbreaking ceremony.

title: Is the world heating up faster than we thought?
content: The article reports on a new study suggesting that global warming may be accelerating rapidly, while highlighting scientific debate over the reliability of short-term climate trends and the urgency of staying below critical temperature thresholds.

title: We study pandemics, and the resurgence of measles is a grim sign of what’s coming
content: The resurgence of measles in the US signals a troubling decline in vaccination rates.

title: Integrated knowledge systems towards flood resilience and sustainable solid waste management in South African urban informal settlements
content: This article outlines how integrating scientific, practitioner and local knowledge systems can strengthen disaster risk reduction—particularly flood resilience and sustainable solid waste management—in South African urban informal settlements.

title: How climate change is fueling disease outbreaks
content: Extreme rainfall and heat driven by climate change triggered Peru’s 2023 dengue outbreak, with 60% of cases linked to cyclone conditions. Researchers warn such weather is now far more likely and call for stronger mosquito control and urban resilience.

title: Yarrabah Aboriginal Shire Council
content: Yarrabah Aboriginal Shire Council will foster unity, respect elders, celebrate courage, facilitate connection, refine for efficiency and acknowledge equality and govern with integrity.

title: Finn Church Aid
content: Finn Church Aid (FCA) is Finland’s largest international aid organisation with more than 70 years of experience.

title: Tunisia scales up urban flood protection efforts with World Bank support
content: The World Bank and the Government of Tunisia are expanding their partnership to strengthen the country’s resilience to climate and disaster risks through an additional financing for the US$ 125 million Tunisia Integrated Disaster Resilience Program.

title: The Lancet Global Health: Modelling suggests climate change could drive millions globally into physical inactivity by 2050 and be linked to an estimated half a million premature deaths
content: Climate change–driven heat could push millions into physical inactivity by 2050, causing up to 700,000 extra premature deaths yearly and billions in productivity losses, especially in hotter low‑ and middle‑income regions.

title: Turning the tide on typhoon havoc in the Philippines
content: Communities in Cagayan, Philippines use anticipatory action—moving boats, securing farms and assets—to reduce typhoon damage. With FAO support, early preparation boosts resilience and speeds recovery from severe storms.

title: Can animals sense earthquakes?
content: Animals often show unusual behaviour before earthquakes, possibly reacting to environmental changes like ions, vibrations or magnetic shifts, but evidence remains mixed and not yet reliable for prediction.

title: Community rapid assessment report: the impact of Cyclone Ditwah on persons with disabilities in Sri Lanka 2025-2026
content: This report presents a rapid assessment of the impacts of a cyclone on persons with disabilities (PwDs) in Sri Lanka, conducted by Yellow Dot and partners.
